libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Keep tests core-only. Use built-in properties/MapPropertySource or injected EnvironmentVariablesPropertySource/SystemPropertiesPropertySource maps. If using real System.setProperty values, set and clear them inside the test. Avoid YAML/JSON/TOML/HOCON examples unless the matching hoplite-* parser module is intentionally added by the test author.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8367 [Automation] native-image run fails for com.sksamuel.hoplite:hoplite-core:3.0.0.RC1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The documentation's common YAML/JSON-style examples require optional hoplite parser modules, but Java .props and in-memory property sources are built into hoplite-core.

Upstream hoplite-core tests use Testcontainers/PostgreSQL for one JdbcHostnameValidator path, but the validator only checks InetAddress reachability, so meaningful coverage can avoid Docker by using loopback/localhost or by testing failure paths.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra deterministic setup is required: hoplite-core 3.0.0.RC1 resolves its runtime dependencies directly, and meaningful core coverage can use built-in MapPropertySource, PropsPropertySource, system properties, environment-variable maps, decoders, preprocessors, and validators without adding parser modules, Docker, or external services.
